description: Evidence-driven comprehensive upgrade of the Sky Delivery project across backend security, order flows, admin reporting, miniapp UX, deployment engineering, and verification evidence. Six agent workstreams coordinated by one main agent.

userStories.title: Comprehensive upgrade control plane and old Ralph migration
userStories.description: As a project coordinator, I want the existing multi-merchant Ralph run archived and absorbed into a comprehensive upgrade loop so that the project has one unified execution control plane.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing scripts/ralph/prd.json and progress.txt are archived under scripts/ralph/archive/2026-05-05-multi-merchant-isolation/ before overwrite.
tasks/prd-sky-delivery-comprehensive-upgrade.md is the source PRD for the new loop.
New Ralph stories US-001..US-008 map to the 6-agent upgrade model.
task_plan.md, findings.md, progress.md, and scripts/ralph/required-gates.md reflect the comprehensive upgrade.
Manual validation: archive folder and new Ralph metadata can be inspected.
userStories.notes: 2026-05-05: Old multi-merchant Ralph state archived to scripts/ralph/archive/2026-05-05-multi-merchant-isolation/ (prd.json, progress.txt, required-gates.md, task_plan.md, findings.md, progress.md). Ralph control-plane regenerated for ralph/sky-delivery-comprehensive-upgrade with stories US-001..US-008.

userStories.title: Backend security and permission boundary upgrade (Agent 1)
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want authentication, authorization, token invalidation, and merchant scope checks to be consistent so that private data cannot be accessed across accounts or merchants.
acceptanceCriteria: Permission matrix documents platform admin, merchant admin, merchant staff, and student user.
Cross-merchant private access for merchant accounts is denied with tests.
Public browsing endpoints remain functional where product rules allow.
Token blacklist behavior remains tested and integrated.
Focused security and scope tests pass.
userStories.notes: 2026-05-05 Agent 1 delivered. MerchantScopeGuard (central guard) created with full Javadoc permission matrix. Methods: requireExplicitMerchantId, resolveAdminQueryMerchantId, resolveMerchantWriteId, assertMerchantAccountCanAccess, assertSameMerchant, requireMerchantContext. Tests: MerchantScopeGuardTest 29/29, MerchantServiceImplScopeTest 2/2, JwtTokenAdminInterceptorTest 8/8, JwtTokenUserInterceptorTest 8/8, TokenBlacklistServiceTest 8/8, TokenBlacklistCleanupTaskTest 2/2. Total 57 tests, 0 failures (BUILD SUCCESS). AccountTypeConstant updated with PLATFORM_ADMIN=1, MERCHANT_ADMIN=2, MERCHANT_STAFF=3, STUDENT_USER=0. Single-merchant fallback preserved via MultiMerchantSchemaSupport.isCoreSchemaReady(). Public browse explicitly skips guard. Risk: MerchantScopeUtils.java retains GBK mojibake on Chinese exception messages — flagged for downstream cleanup.

userStories.title: Order and transaction flow upgrade (Agent 2)
userStories.description: As a student user and merchant operator, I want cart, order, payment-state, reorder, and reminder flows to stay consistent so that orders cannot mix merchants or lose transactional integrity.
acceptanceCriteria: Private cart operations use explicit merchant context in multi-merchant mode.
Order submit validates same-merchant cart, dish, setmeal, order, and order detail data.
Reorder, cancel, history, detail, and reminder paths preserve user and merchant ownership.
Transactional behavior on order creation and cart cleanup is verified.
Cart and order service tests pass.
userStories.notes: 2026-05-05 Agent 2 delivered. ShoppingCartServiceImpl and OrderServiceImpl already correctly integrate MerchantScopeGuard (requireExplicitMerchantId, assertSameMerchant, assertMerchantAccountCanAccess). Audit confirmed: cart bound to userId+merchantId, order submit validates same-merchant, reorder uses original-order merchant, cancel/detail/history check user ownership, transactional rollback on cross-merchant attempts. Tests: ShoppingCartServiceImplTest (17 new tests) + OrderServiceImplTest (extended to 21 tests, 14 new). Total 42 tests in *Order*Test,*ShoppingCart*Test, 0 failures (BUILD SUCCESS). Legacy single-merchant fallback preserved. Mock WeChat Pay branch unchanged.

userStories.title: Admin reporting and workspace upgrade (Agent 3)
userStories.description: As a platform or merchant operator, I want management screens and statistics to respect my role scope so that reports and dashboard data are accurate and not overbroad.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin endpoints distinguish platform-global and merchant-scoped semantics.
Report metrics (turnover, order count, user statistics, sales Top10, export) honor scope.
Workspace metrics (business, order, dish, setmeal overview) honor scope.
Merchant accounts cannot view global statistics.
Report/workspace/admin tests or documented API checks verify both perspectives.
userStories.notes: 2026-05-05 Agent 3 delivered. ReportServiceImpl and WorkspaceServiceImpl thread merchantScopeGuard.resolveAdminQueryMerchantId(null) into every metric call (turnover, user, order, top10, businessData, orderOverView, dishOverView, setmealOverView, Excel export). Excel export reuses workspaceService.getBusinessData → same scope filter; merchant admin cannot export competitor 30-day window. Tests: ReportServiceImplScopeTest 8/8, WorkspaceServiceImplScopeTest 7/7. Combined Maven run (*Report*Test,*Workspace*Test,*Scope*Test) 46 tests, 0 failures (BUILD SUCCESS). Admin contract documented at core/nginx/html/sky/merchant-admin/admin-api-scope.md (account-type matrix, endpoint scope table, single-merchant fallback note, frontend assumptions, verification command).

userStories.title: Miniapp UX and API contract upgrade (Agent 4)
userStories.description: As a student user, I want merchant selection, menu browsing, cart, address, order, and payment flows to behave consistently so that ordering from campus merchants feels reliable.
acceptanceCriteria: Miniapp private requests consistently carry merchantId or compatible shopId.
Public browse endpoints remain usable without login when appropriate.
401/session handling and user-facing errors reviewed.
Cart and order UI flows align with backend request contracts.
Manual or E2E verification covers core user flows.
userStories.notes: 2026-05-05 Agent 4 delivered. Audit confirmed withMerchantScope correctly injects merchantId for cart/order/category/dish/setmeal/shop/merchant-info APIs. Reorder uses resolveMerchantIdFromOrder; historyOrder shows correct merchant. 401 redirect clears session and redirects to login modal which restores default merchant. One real bug fixed: removed duplicate getData() definition in core/miniapp/pages/index/index.js (shadowed earlier method). Risks documented: (1) request.js currently login-gates ALL endpoints (including shop/category/dish listings) — public anonymous browse not supported in current miniapp design, flagged as product/policy decision; (2) core/miniapp/utils/webscoket.js is dead code (legacy training URL wss://socket-canzg.itheima.net/ws, no imports); (3) 401 redirect strips in-flight merchantId route param. None of these are blocking; recommended for future cleanup.

userStories.title: Deployment, environment, and engineering upgrade (Agent 5)
userStories.description: As a developer or deployer, I want clear setup, migration, startup, and deployment documentation so that the project can be reproduced on a new machine without guessing.
acceptanceCriteria: README and SETUP describe Java, Maven, MySQL, Redis, Node, Nginx, ports, and default accounts.
Environment templates contain placeholders, not real secrets.
Database initialization and multi-merchant migration order are documented.
Docker/Compose and health-check design is available or implemented.
Temporary files and generated artifacts avoid C drive.
userStories.notes: 2026-05-05 Agent 5 delivered. README/SETUP/.env.example/tooling/.env.example/scripts/start-all.bat/scripts/stop-all.bat already comprehensive (audit confirmed Java 17, Maven, MySQL 8.0, Redis 6+, Node 18, Nginx 1.24, ports 8080/8081/3306/6379, default admin account, mock-login flag, JWT length sanity check, port pre-check, health probe via /internal/health). Created two missing referenced docs: docs/deployment/troubleshooting.md (338 lines: JWT length, Redis auth, MySQL credentials, schema mismatch, port conflicts, Nginx start failure, miniapp LAN, health probe, WeChat Pay) and docs/deployment/docker.md (177 lines: service inventory, Compose layout, named-vs-bind volumes for D-drive, secrets via --env-file, init-script ordering, health checks, validation checklist marked NOT YET RUN). Docker artifacts preserved at core/backend/deploy/{Dockerfile,docker-compose.yml,.dockerignore}. No real credentials. UTF-8 valid throughout. Risk: docker compose up has not been validated against this repo — checklist must be run before promoting Docker status from 'proposed' to 'verified'.

userStories.title: Verification system and quality evidence upgrade (Agent 6)
userStories.description: As a project reviewer, I want a quality evidence matrix so that I can see which tests passed, which were skipped, and which risks remain.
acceptanceCriteria: Feynman 2026-05-05 reports are summarized into an upgrade input matrix.
Maven, Playwright, API, manual, and Feynman-derived checks classified.
TIMEOUT/PARTIAL/SKIP cases have documented interpretation and follow-up strategy.
Required gates define pass evidence for all six agent lines.
Final report template is ready to be filled.
userStories.notes: 2026-05-05 Agent 6 delivered. docs/reviews/2026-05-05-comprehensive-upgrade-verification-matrix.md inventoried 22 Java test classes (1 sky-common + 21 sky-server) and 3 Playwright spec files (8 test cases). Feynman 2026-05-05 absorption: 17 PASS / 2 PARTIAL / 0 FAIL / 4 TIMEOUT / 17 SKIP. Tool-vs-project decision table: zero rows map to a Sky Delivery defect — all 17 SKIP rows are Feynman v0.2.40 tool/env limitations (REPL-only slash commands + missing CLIs + out-of-scope spec); all 4 TIMEOUT rows are model-latency / rate-limit. Public browse regression row included. Coordinator regression sweep: 5-step plan in section 6 of matrix. Coverage gaps tagged with agent owners. Project test rows default to PENDING until coordinator sweep transitions them with evidence.

userStories.title: Six-line integration, regression verification, and final upgrade report
userStories.description: As a project owner, I want all six upgrade lines integrated and summarized so that the upgraded project has a coherent final result.
acceptanceCriteria: All agent delivery summaries are collected.
File conflicts are resolved by the coordinator.
Required gates pass or have explicit blockers with owners.
Final project upgrade report lists changes, test evidence, known limitations, and next steps.
Relevant tests pass or failures are documented with root cause and follow-up.
userStories.notes: 2026-05-05: Agents 1-6 all returned with delivery summaries. Coordinator collected evidence and updated US-002..US-007. Pending: final regression sweep + final report rendering from docs/reviews/2026-05-05-comprehensive-upgrade-final-report-template.md.
